name: Lab Interpretation — Cirrhosis Multi-System Panel
description: Level 3 — MIMIC hadm 22987108 — renal + liver + hematology + electrolytes in decompensated cirrhosis
prompt:
Interpret the following laboratory results for a 69-year-old male with known liver cirrhosis (NASH) and type 2 diabetes, admitted in shock with hematemesis:

Renal:
- BUN: 49 mg/dL (reference: 6–20 mg/dL)
- Creatinine: 2.1 mg/dL (reference: 0.5–1.2 mg/dL)

Liver:
- Total Bilirubin: 12.3 mg/dL (reference: 0–1.5 mg/dL)
- ALT: 205 IU/L (reference: 0–40 IU/L)
- AST: 227 IU/L (reference: 0–40 IU/L)

Hematology:
- WBC: 15,100 /uL (reference: 4,000–10,000)
- Platelets: 78,000 /uL (reference: 150,000–400,000)
- Hemoglobin: 9.7 g/dL (reference: 13.7–17.5 g/dL)

Electrolytes / Acid-base:
- Sodium: 128 mEq/L (reference: 133–145 mEq/L)
- Bicarbonate: 16 mEq/L (reference: 22–32 mEq/L)
- Anion Gap: 28 mEq/L (reference: 8–20 mEq/L)
- Chloride: 95 mEq/L (reference: 96–108 mEq/L)

Provide clinical interpretation of these results.

abnormal_values.significance: Severe jaundice indicating profound hepatic failure and/or biliary obstruction
Hepatocellular injury; AST > ALT ratio suggests alcohol or cirrhotic etiology
Elevated creatinine in context of cirrhosis — raises suspicion for hepatorenal syndrome
Markedly elevated; combined with upper GI bleed suggests protein load contribution
Thrombocytopenia from hypersplenism (portal hypertension) and/or DIC
Leukocytosis consistent with sepsis (E. coli bacteremia)
Anemia from combination of hemorrhage, hypersplenism, and chronic liver disease
Severe hyponatremia in cirrhosis — hypervolemic hyponatremia from impaired free water excretion
Metabolic acidosis from lactic acidosis in shock state
Markedly elevated — high anion gap metabolic acidosis consistent with severe lactic acidosis from septic/hypovolemic shock

expected.interpretation: Decompensated cirrhosis with multi-system failure: hepatic failure (bilirubin 12.3, moderate transaminase elevation), acute kidney injury suspicious for hepatorenal syndrome (Cr 2.1 + cirrhosis), severe sepsis (leukocytosis + bacteremia), high anion gap metabolic acidosis from shock, hyponatremia from hepatic ascites/hypervolemia, thrombocytopenia from portal hypertension and/or DIC.
expected.pattern_recognition: Decompensated cirrhosis cascade: bilirubin 12.3 + AST:ALT ratio >1 (227:205) = hepatic failure + portal hypertension → thrombocytopenia (78K) + hyponatremia (128) + ascites. Superimposed sepsis: leukocytosis + shock → lactic acidosis (AG 28, bicarb 16) + AKI (Cr 2.1, BUN 49) + possible hepatorenal syndrome.
differential_considerations: Hepatorenal syndrome type 1 (rapidly progressive AKI in cirrhosis)
E. coli sepsis with septic shock causing multi-organ failure
